channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: The Most Important Question for UN Secretary-General Candidates: Our Survey Results
item.description: This week, PassBlue produced an informal survey asking our readers to submit what they considered the most important question they would ask the current UN secretary-general candidates if they had a chance to do so. The anonymous responses, submitted through our emailed survey, held July 19-21, revealed a portrait of an institution under strain and ... Read more

item.title: Children in Conflict Zones and Remote Areas Bear the Brunt of Global
item.description: Despite growing up and working in vastly different geographic regions and environments — one shaped by decades of armed conflict and political instability, and the other by remote, hard-to-reach areas — both Sagal and Johao encounter children daily who have never been vaccinated. Sagal and Johao are members of UNICEF’s Global Youth Health Advisory Group, […]

item.title: Security Council LIVE: Can critical minerals fuel peace instead of conflict?
item.description: The Security Council meets this morning to debate critical minerals — the lithium, cobalt and copper powering the clean energy transition. Demand is set to triple by 2030. The question: will this mineral wealth build peace and prosperity, or fund conflict and corruption? Follow live updates throughout the day.

item.title: From lithium to nickel: How the UN is helping countries make the most of critical minerals
item.description: The world's shift to clean energy is driving huge demand for critical minerals such as lithium, nickel, cobalt and copper.

item.title: Deadly Afghanistan floods kill at least 26 as aid agencies race to reach survivors
item.description: Flash floods have killed at least 26 people across eastern Afghanistan, including 23 in the mountainous province of Nuristan, where homes, markets and roads were swept away and search teams continued looking for people reported missing.

item.title: World News in Brief: Aid for Ukraine, drone attacks in Sudan, DR Congo deaths, neurological disorders in the Americas
item.description: UN partners launched the Winter Response Plan to help 2.1 million people in Ukraine survive another cold season as the country grapples with the on-going Russian invasion.

item.title: Nicaragua continues to weaken political freedoms, UN warns
item.description: The UN human rights chief Volker Türk on Wednesday condemned new threats from Nicaragua’s co-president Daniel Ortega to hinder civil rights and prevent dissenting individuals and groups from participating in elections.

item.title: Thousands prevented from returning home to southern Lebanon
item.description: As months of conflict continue to devastate communities across Lebanon, families are being left without shelter, healthcare and other essential services.

item.title: Africa’s Health Defenses Have Improved But Its Funding Has Not
item.description: Against the backdrop of an Ebola disease outbreak, the new Africa Health Security (AHS) Index, released today, reveals significant gaps in critical health infrastructure despite important progress in health security capacity in recent years.

item.title: US Plans to Restrict Visas to Foreign Journalists Come Under Fire
item.description: A proposal to restrict US visas to foreign journalists assigned to cover the US has triggered a strong protest from the New York-based Foreign Press Association (FPA). The FPA says it is dismayed by the US administration’s proposals for restricting international press visas. The shortened duration of the press “I” visa would make it impossible […]

item.title: Africa Can Grow Faster With AI—If It Moves Now
item.description: A farmer in Kenya gets weather and planting advice on a basic phone. A teacher in Nigeria uses a chatbot to help students catch up in math. South Africa’s revenue authority uses data analytics to better target tax audits. These are not futuristic examples from Silicon Valley. They are early signs of the broader transformation […]

item.title: ALBANIA: ‘The Public Only Learns about These Development Megaprojects Once Construction Machinery Arrives’
item.description: CIVICUS discusses land grabs and environmental damage linked to a luxury coastal resort project with Eva Kushova, Director of Destination Management Organisation Albania, a civil society organisation that supports sustainable tourism in Albanian destinations, seeking to preserve culture and nature while maximising benefits for communities. Since May, protests have rocked Albania over a luxury […]
